# policies()

**Instance Method**

Returns an array of policies used to evaluate the status of the displayed certificates.

## Declaration

```swift
func policies() -> [Any]!
```

## Discussion

This method returns an autoreleased [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSArray] containing one or more objects of type [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Security/SecPolicy] , as set by a previous [doc://com.apple.securityinterface/documentation/SecurityInterface/SFChooseIdentityPanel/setPolicies(_:)] call, or the Apple X.509 Basic Policy if [doc://com.apple.securityinterface/documentation/SecurityInterface/SFChooseIdentityPanel/setPolicies(_:)] has not been called. See [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Security/certificate-key-and-trust-services] in [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Security/certificate-key-and-trust-services] for a list of policies and object identifiers provided by the [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/Security/Conceptual/cryptoservices/CDSA/CDSA.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40011172-CH4-CHDEEDBE].

## Customizing the Appearance of the Sheet or Panel

- **setAlternateButtonTitle(_:)**: Customizes the title of the alternate button.
- **setDefaultButtonTitle(_:)**: Customizes the title of the default button.
- **setPolicies(_:)**: Specifies one or more policies that apply to the displayed certificates.
- **informativeText()**: Returns the informative text currently displayed in the panel.
- **setInformativeText(_:)**: Sets the optional informative text displayed in the panel.
